turn your
brain off
at night.

Root Cause

Elevated nighttime cortisol is preventing your nervous system from downshifting. Your body knows it's tired. Your brain won't let go. That's not a willpower problem — that's biochemistry with a clear correction.

Clinical Oversight
Dr. Felicia
Stoler
DCN · MS · RDN · FACSM · FAND · Dipl ACLM

Dr. Stoler holds a Doctorate in Clinical Nutrition (DCN) with deep specialization in the relationship between nutritional biochemistry and sleep architecture. As a Registered Dietitian Nutritionist and Fellow of the American College of Sports Medicine, she understands sleep not as a behavior problem but as a systemic regulation failure — one correctable through targeted clinical intervention.

She serves as Principal Investigator for Jazz Bird 501(c)(3) and maintains an active Fullscript clinical dispensary. Every BLEU sleep protocol is personally reviewed against your medication profile before recommendation.

The Mechanism

Why your cortisol
won't stand down.

What should happen
Cortisol Curve

Cortisol peaks at 6–8am to initiate waking. It should reach its lowest point between 10pm–2am — the window your brain uses to initiate deep, restorative sleep cycles.

What's broken
The Flatline Problem

Chronic stress, blue light after sunset, irregular eating, and systemic inflammation all flatten or invert the curve. Night cortisol stays elevated. The nervous system reads danger. Sleep cannot begin.

The correction
The Reset Window

The Sleep Reset Protocol targets magnesium deficiency (depleted by cortisol itself), adenosine pressure restoration, and GABA upregulation — the three levers that force the cortisol curve back into rhythm.

The Protocol

Sleep Reset Protocol
30 days. One outcome: you sleep.

Week 1
Days 1–7
Cortisol Floor

Magnesium glycinate 300mg at 8:30pm. Light protocol. No screens 90 minutes before bed. Establish the downshift window.

Week 2
Days 8–14
Nervous System

L-theanine 200mg added at 9:30pm. Adaptogen support for HPA axis. Ashwagandha cortisol suppression protocol begins.

Week 3
Days 15–21
Architecture

Sleep cycle quality monitoring. Deep sleep proportion increases. Glycine 3g for core temperature drop. REM rebound period.

Week 4
Days 22–30
Locked In

Rhythm is set. ALVAI check-in at Day 30. Protocol continues or pivots based on your reported outcome. One next step only.

The Outcome
Day 30: you know
if this worked.

Not a vague "better sleep." A specific, measurable shift: time to sleep onset, number of wake events, morning energy score. ALVAI tracks all three from Day 1. If the protocol didn't land — we adjust. There is always a next protocol.

222 Cities · Sleep Protocol

Your city context
is part of your protocol.

Chronic stress is not the same in every city. Shift work in Chicago is different from humidity-wrecked sleep in New Orleans. Your ALVAI greeting reflects your city. Your practitioners are local. Your protocol is yours.

New Orleans
neworleans.bleu.live/sleep

"New Orleans heals through music and second lines — but the city carries one of the highest chronic stress burdens in the South. Your sleep protocol starts here."

→ 3 verified sleep specialists · NOLA
Atlanta
atlanta.bleu.live/sleep

"Atlanta moves fast. The hustle culture of the South's economic engine runs on cortisol. Your sleep protocol is the reset your nervous system has been asking for."

→ 5 verified sleep specialists · ATL
Chicago
chicago.bleu.live/sleep

"Chicago winters create a circadian disruption cycle that compounds cortisol dysregulation. Shift workers on the L run the highest sleep debt in the Midwest."
